tool.name: GFAffix
tool.description: Collapse walk-preserving shared affixes in a variation graph
tool.command:
#set $in_ext = 'gfa.gz' if str($input.ext) == 'gfa1.gz' else 'gfa' ln -sf '$input' 'input.${in_ext}' &&
gfaffix 'input.${in_ext}' --output_refined '$refined_graph' #if $output_affixes --output_affixes '$affixes_tsv' #end if #if $output_transformation --output_transformation '$transformation_tsv' #end if $check_transformation #if str($dont_collapse).strip() --dont_collapse '$dont_collapse' #end if --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}"

param.name: input
param.label: Input variation graph (GFA1)
param.help: GFA1 format, plain or gzipped. Typically the smoothed GFA from seqwish/smoothxg.

param.name: output_affixes
param.label: Report identified affixes
param.help: Emits a TSV of every collapsed shared prefix/suffix.

param.name: output_transformation
param.label: Report node transformation map
param.help: For every original node, the walk in the refined graph that replaces it.

param.label: Verify sequence preservation
param.help: Checks that transformed sub-graphs spell identical sequences to the original. Slower but catches regressions.

param.label: Do-not-collapse path regex
param.help: Skip nodes on P/W lines whose name matches this regular expression. Leave blank to collapse everywhere.

tool.help:
**GFAffix** — collapse walk-preserving shared affixes in a variation graph.
After `seqwish` + `smoothxg`, the pangenome graph often has nodes that share identical prefixes or suffixes across walks. GFAffix collapses those without changing the underlying paths' spelled sequences, producing a smaller, less redundant graph.
This is the canonical post-smoothing tidy step in the `pggb` pipeline.
-----
**Inputs**
- `Input variation graph` — GFA1 from seqwish/smoothxg, plain or gzipped.
**Outputs**
- `Refined graph` — collapsed GFA1. - `Collapsed affixes` (optional) — TSV listing every shared prefix/suffix that was merged. Empty file if nothing was collapsed. - `Node transformation` (optional) — per-original-node map of the walk that replaces it.
**Options**
- `--check_transformation` — paranoid sequence-preservation check. - `--dont_collapse` — regex over P/W line names; skip collapsing nodes touched by matching paths.
-----
GFAffix is part of the pangenome pipeline (`pggb` runs it internally), but is also useful as a standalone post-processing step on any GFA1 graph.
